intro:
There is no information available on the plant Goeppertia portobelensis. However, I can provide information on the plant Goeppertia, which is more commonly known as Calathea or Goeppertia. 

Goeppertia, formerly known as Calathea, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Marantaceae. They are popular ornamental plants native to tropical regions of the Americas.

The health benefits of Goeppertia are not well-documented, but some species of the Calathea genus have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation. However, more research is needed to confirm their efficacy.

The bioactive constituents of Goeppertia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to their potential health benefits. However, the exact composition may vary depending on the specific species and growing conditions.

The most common herbal preparations made with Goeppertia are teas, infusions, and tinctures, although these are not widely available commercially due to the limited scientific research and lack of standardization.
